channel.title: Benjy School Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Benjy School Notes

item.description: The opportunity Exposition Timmy steals Jemma’s Justin Bieber body pillow and wets the bed and it is ruined Jemma very angry Family goes on holiday to USA Timmy takes the window seat Complication Jemma gets an opportunity to push her little brother into the grand canyon.
Apostrophes Contractions Can not > can’t Will not > won’t It is > it’s Possessive Luke’s house Emily’s computer Exception: The dog was licking its paw.
The Opportunity The lock clicked as my dad turned the key. He opened the door and I took my bag up to my room to unpack.
D - Direct Address A - Alliteration F - Facts O - Opinions R - Repetition R - Rhetorical Questions E - Emotive Language S - Statistics T - Triplets.
Proceed with Purpose—Writing about Non-fiction PEWE Point Evidence Writer Explain The writer presents ideas about how to save the environment through becoming a Womble.
(Recycling) reduces the the consumption of raw materials and fossil fuels.
Proceed with Purpose Identify purpose Identify the writer’s ideas Identify the how ⇒ DAFOREST techniques .

Q6: How does the writer present ideas about climate change? Identify the writers point(s) People are not realising how big the issue is It will affect the whole world in a very bad way We need to act now, it may already be too late Find Techniques in quotes Our planet is our home, the place that sus...

Question 6 Again How does the writer present ideas about speaking with cats? Steps: What is the writer’s purpose? 3 or 4 of their ideas? How do they do step 1? (methods/techniques/DAFOREST) The writer is entertaining and informing the reader.

Comparing TambourineBothSteel DrumJinglesMake soundPercusionHand heldWoodSteel Quote Emily says talk about purpose Question 7 Compare how writers of Text One and text Two present ideas and perspectives on climate change Text 1 (Isha)BothText 2 (Greta)Not as stronglyInformingFocused on destructionStr...
